event: The Ecstasy of Saint Catherine of Siena
symbols: crown of thorns on her head — Catherine's mystical participation in Christ's Passion
stigmata visible on hands — the wounds of Christ imprinted on the mystic's body
crucifix in her hand — her total devotion to Christ crucified
lily held by angel — purity and virginal consecration
closed eyes and swooning posture — the ecstatic union with God that transcends the senses
supporting angels — the heavenly assistance during mystical transport
description: A half-length image showing a woman in a Dominican habit — black veil over white robes — with her eyes closed in ecstasy, her head tilted back and supported by two curly-haired angels with wings. She wears a crown of thorns and holds a small crucifix. Red marks of stigmata are visible on her hands. One angel at left gazes tenderly at her while the other at right holds what appears to be a lily. The background is a warm golden glow. The painting has rich, warm tones with the white Dominican habit contrasting against the golden backdrop.
